item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Incredibly weak, formulaic, boring, unimaginative and it even has a misused mythological being. I would recommend to scrap the idea and do something else entirely. Downvoted.

Sounds harsh, really, but I just want to point out that all other of Soulless' works have agreed with me and this one was surprisingly lacksluster. You can do better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted as it is effective even though its shorter than most. Also, let him get his tree! ;-;

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1 for "[quieter] Okay."

That reminded me of this bird: http://vine-avenue.com/hey-thats-my-money-%f0%9f%98%81/

he just looks so disappointed ;-;

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really liked this. It's a squirrel. It's a squirrel that's smart enough to fulfill his function. But it's still a squirrel.

Smart-ass quibble: "No text messages, calls, letters or similar messages may be written within 5 meters of SCP-2707." I am trying to figure out how to write a phone call outside of using a TTY.

+1 for "[quieter] Okay."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank's for considering my suggestion.

It is certainly weird this squirrel is speaking only Malay but he knows the Norse name Ratatoskr. It can be start point of something creative.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That sounds interesting. I'll think about rewriting this appropriately.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have a suggestion.

Why doesn't doctor ask about meaning of title―Ratatoskr? In Norse language, Ratatoskr means "drill-tooth" or "bore-tooth." If any foundation personnel who knows about Norse mythology read this article, she can be wonder what relation about this Norse name and 2707's claim.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At first, I downvoted primarily because of codename(Ratatoskr).

But now, after rename codename to "The Ratatoskr Lineage", it makes sense and is pleasing alteration. I rerated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted on account of both being a descendent of a named mythological wildlife character (as opposed to a deity or humanoid) & being uncensored in presentation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The squirrel speaks Malay because the Prevost's Squirrel resides in areas that speak Malay. As for why a Southeast Asian squirrel is claiming to be Ratatoskr's next in line, well, let's just say he knew how to get around.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait, I thought I was the one who wrote all the talking animal SCPs. Are you me, Soulless?

Seriously, though, it's cute, I'm decidedly curious about why the squirrel speaks Malay, and I hope the great tree is still out there somewhere so he can climb it and make his mama proud.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Midway through the description, I was like "It's ratatosk, isn't it."

I was proven right. I do agree this could use more meat, but I'm willing to +1 regardless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm inclined to agree. Without knowing what Ratatoskr is, it's just some message-delivering squirrel. After learning about Ratatoskr, I feel like more could have been done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just don't like it. This feels like a massive waste of the ratatoskr's potential. Of all the ways you could use it, this is arguably the most generic and bland. So-so anomalous effects that aren't expanded upon and an interview that boils down to 'this is a/the Ratatoskr!' and the 'the SCP wants to go home' trope.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Bleh, another talking animal Scip and a very transparent reference to Norse Mythology, at that.
Negative vote, for now at least.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Editing appropriately, thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
These are to go ignored.

This ought to go in the containment procedures. Otherwise, I think I like this, and it's the interview that sells it. Poor little guy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For Reference And Your Consideration.

Also, upvoted. :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Are you going to boot me out of wiki-isms?

"Congratulations Soulless, we're promoting you to admin!"
"Great!"
"Of Forum only!"
"…uh…"

EDIT: I just want to finish as many ideas as I can before moving onto my novella. Ideally, all my 50k will be on the site, but the chances of that happening are slim to none.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Soulless tries to make a super short SCP to see what would happen.

For NaNoWriMo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Soulless, at this point, my reaction is perfectly expressed by the following video:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=faMh6OYfuNE

Now, (half-)seriously. This one was merely OK compared to your last couple, but your rapid fire strategy is definitely an interesting experiment (albeit one which may summon Kalinin's rage). Got any set goal in terms of skips/word count before dedicating fully to your novella?
